docs.title: Introduction
docs.text: Make cool terminal animations without the pain. Move text around, use RGB colors, play audio, and build actual animations. Works great on modern terminals.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
• Particle simulation
  • The zen of python
  • Audio example
  • Loading screen
  • Tag game
  • Collision example
  • Player Controls
  • Linear interpolation

docs.title: Install
docs.text: pip install animpy

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
import animpy

scene = animpy.Scene()
text = animpy.Text("Hello!", 10, 5, r=255, g=100, b=50)
scene.add(text)
scene.render()

docs.title: Comparison to other libraries
docs.text: Feature Animpy Curses Blessings Rich Animated text ✅ ❌ ❌ ❌ RGB color support ✅ ❌ ❌ ✅ Audio playback ✅ ❌ ❌ ❌ Easy to use ✅ ❌ ❌ ✅

docs.title: Examples of other libraries
docs.text:
• Curses: A low-level library for terminal handling, but it doesn't support animations or RGB colors.
  • Blessings: A wrapper around curses that makes it easier to use, but still lacks animation and audio features.
  • Rich: A modern library for rich text and formatting in the terminal, but it doesn't support animations or audio.
  • Animpy: A high-level library designed specifically for creating terminal animations with RGB colors and audio support, making it easy to create dynamic and engaging terminal applications.

docs.title: Curses example
docs.text:
import curses
def main(stdscr):
    curses.curs_set(0)  # Hide cursor
    stdscr.addstr(5, 10, "Hello, Curses!", curses.color_pair(1))
    stdscr.refresh()
    stdscr.getch()  # Wait for key press
curses.wrapper(main)

docs.title: Blessings example
docs.text:
from blessings import Terminal
term = Terminal()

print(term.move(5, 10) + term.red("Hello, Blessings!"))

docs.title: Rich example
docs.text:
from rich import print
print("[red]Hello, Rich![/red]")

docs.title: Animpy example
docs.text:
import animpy
scene = animpy.Scene()
text = animpy.Text("Hello, Animpy!", 10, 5, r=255, g=100, b=50)
scene.add(text)
scene.render()

docs.title: Pros
docs.text:
• Easy to create animated text with RGB colors
  • Built-in audio support for adding sound effects and music
  • Simple API for moving text and creating animations
  • Works well on modern terminals with good performance

docs.title: Cons
docs.text:
• May not work properly on older terminals that lack RGB support
  • Limited to text-based animations (no images or graphics)
  • Performance may degrade with a large number of animated objects
  • Still in early development, so some features may be missing or buggy

docs.title: Performance
docs.text: Animpy is designed to be efficient and runs smoothly on modern terminals. The performance will depend on the complexity of the animation and the capabilities of the terminal, but in general, it should be able to handle multiple animated text objects without significant lag. For best performance, it's recommended to keep the number of animated objects reasonable and to use the dt property for smooth movement based on frame timing. Future improvements could include optimizing the rendering loop and adding support for more complex animations while maintaining good performance.

docs.title: Todo list for future improvements
docs.text:
• Add support for more complex animations (e.g., sprite sheets, particle systems)
  • Implement a more robust audio system with volume control and multiple channels
  • Optimize rendering for better performance with many animated objects
  • Add more built-in animation effects (e.g., fade in/out, bounce)

docs.title: Methods list
docs.text:
Text – Create animated text:

text = animpy.Text("Hi", 0, 0, r=255, g=0, b=0)
text.moveX(10)  # Move right
text.moveY(5)   # Move down
text.centerX()  # Center horizontally
text.change_rgb_values(0, 255, 0)  # Change color
text.collides_with(other_text)  # Check collision with another text
text.width, text.height  # Get dimensions
text.type_out("Type me!", speed=0.05, scene=scene)  # Type effect
text.fall(velocity=2, floor=15)  # Falling effect
text.change_frame()  # Cycle through frames (if you used a list)

Scene – Render everything:

scene = animpy.Scene()
scene.add(text1, text2, text3)
scene.remove(text2)  # Remove text2 from the scene
scene.render()
scene.set_bg_rgb(0, 0, 255)  # Set background color to blue
scene.clear()
scene.shake(intensity=2)  # Shake the scene
scene.dt  # Get time since last frame (for smooth movement)

Interactive Scene – Handle real-time input:

scene = animpy.Scene()
scene.add(text1, text2, text3)
scene.remove(text2)  # Remove text2 from the scene
scene.render()
scene.set_bg_rgb(0, 0, 255)  # Set background color to blue
scene.clear()
scene.shake(intensity=2)  # Shake the scene
scene.key_pressed("w")  # Check if 'w' is pressed
scene.dt  # Get time since last frame (for smooth movement)

Audio – Play sounds:

audio = animpy.Audio()
audio.load("bg", "music.mp3")
audio.play("bg", loop=-1)
audio.stop_all()
audio.is_playing("track")

Animpy (extras) – Some extra methods for animations:

animpy.lerp(start, end, t)  # Linear interpolation between start and end

docs.title: Method instructions
docs.text: Here are the instructions for all the methods in animpy, with code examples.

docs.title: animpy.Text() - Create a text object with optional RGB color and z-index for layering:
docs.text: text = animpy.Text("Hi", 0, 0, r=255, g=0, b=0, z_index=1)

docs.title: moveX and moveY - Move text horizontally or vertically:
docs.text:
text.moveX(10)  # Move right
text.moveY(5)   # Move down

docs.title: centerX - Center text horizontally on the scene:
docs.text: text.centerX()

docs.title: change_rgb_values - Change the text color:
docs.text: text.change_rgb_values(0, 255, 0)  # Change color to green

docs.title: collides_with - Check if this text collides with another text:
docs.text: text.collides_with(other_text)

docs.title: width and height - Get the dimensions of the text:
docs.text: text.width, text.height

docs.title: type_out - Create a typewriter effect for the text:
docs.text: text.type_out("Type me!", speed=0.05, scene=scene)

docs.title: fall - Make the text fall with gravity:
docs.text: text.fall(velocity=2, floor=15)

docs.title: change_frame - Cycle through frames if the text was created with a list of strings:
docs.text:
text.change_frame()

Scene – Render everything:

docs.title: animpy.Scene() - Create a new scene:
docs.text: scene = animpy.Scene()

docs.title: add and remove - Add or remove items from the scene:
docs.text:
scene.add(text1, text2, text3)
scene.remove(text2)

docs.title: render - Render the scene to the terminal:
docs.text: scene.render()

docs.title: set_bg_rgb - Set the background color of the scene:
docs.text: scene.set_bg_rgb(0, 0, 255)  # Set background color to blue

docs.title: clear - Clear the scene:
docs.text: scene.clear()

docs.title: shake - Shake the scene for a dramatic effect:
docs.text: scene.shake(intensity=2)

docs.title: dt - Get the time since the last frame (useful for smooth movement):
docs.text:
scene.dt

Interactive Scene – Handle real-time input:

docs.title: animpy.InteractiveScene() - Create a new interactive scene:
docs.text: scene = animpy.InteractiveScene()

docs.title: key_pressed - Check if a specific key is currently pressed:
docs.text:
scene.key_pressed("w")  # Check if 'w' is pressed

Audio – Play sounds:

docs.title: animpy.Audio() - Create a new audio manager:
docs.text: audio = animpy.Audio()

docs.title: load - Load an audio file with a specific name:
docs.text: audio.load("bg", "music.mp3")

docs.title: play - Play a loaded audio track, with optional looping:
docs.text: audio.play("bg", loop=-1)

docs.title: stop_all - Stop all currently playing audio:
docs.text: audio.stop_all()

docs.title: is_playing - Check if a specific track is currently playing:
docs.text:
audio.is_playing("track")

Animpy (extras) – Some extra methods for animations:

docs.title: lerp - Linear interpolation between two values:
docs.text: animpy.lerp(start, end, t)  # Returns a value between start and end based on t (0 to 1)

docs.title: Simple animation example:
docs.text:
import animpy
scene = animpy.Scene()
text = animpy.Text("Hello, Animpy!", 10, 5, r=255, g=100, b=50)
scene.add(text)
for _ in range(20):
    text.moveX(1)  # Move right
    text.moveY(0.5)  # Move down
    scene.render()

docs.title: Conclusion
docs.text: Animpy is a powerful and easy-to-use library for creating terminal animations with RGB colors and audio support. It provides a simple API for moving text, creating animations, and handling real-time input, making it a great choice for anyone looking to add some flair to their terminal applications. With good performance on modern terminals and a growing list of features, animpy is a fantastic tool for both beginners and experienced developers alike.

docs.title: v1.5.0
docs.text:
• Added linear interpolation function lerp to animpy for smooth animations
  • Added dt property to Scene for easy frame timing and smooth movement
  • Added z-index support to Text for layering items in the scene
  • Added scene shaking effect with shake method for dramatic animations

docs.title: v1.4.5
docs.text: • Added background color support to Scene with set_bg_rgb method

docs.title: v1.4.1
docs.text: • Added scene.remove method to remove items from the scene

docs.title: v1.4.0
docs.text:
• Added three new examples to Github example folder
  • Added new class InteractiveScene that allows for real-time keyboard input
  • Added collision detection method collides_with to Text class

docs.title: v1.3.8
docs.text:
• Added audio.is_playing() method to check if audio is currently playing
  • Added two more examples to Github example folder

docs.title: v1.3.5
docs.text:
• Added scene.clear method to clear the scene
  • Added version history to README
